source_paper_title: Convergence Analysis of Accelerated Stochastic Gradient Descent Under the Growth Condition
source_paper_bibtex: @article{Chen_2024, title={Convergence Analysis of Accelerated Stochastic Gradient Descent Under the Growth Condition}, volume={49}, ISSN={1526-5471}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1287/moor.2021.0293}, DOI={10.1287/moor.2021.0293}, number={4}, journal={Mathematics of Operations Research}, publisher={Institute for Operations Research and the Management Sciences (INFORMS)}, author={Chen, You-Lin and Na, Sen and Kolar, Mladen}, year={2024}, month=nov, pages={2492–2526} }
context:
Page 27, Section 7 (Conclusion)

We do not provide a lower bound analysis for NAM, RMM, iDAM+, and, therefore, cannot conclude that these methods do not improve the convergence over SGD when \(\delta \ge 1\). ... One of the limitations of our work is the sharpness of the analysis. Due to the lack of lower bound analysis on \(\delta\), it is unclear whether the condition of having a small \(\delta\) for different methods is necessary for acceleration; and how sharp such a condition is.
title: Lower bounds on acceleration thresholds for stochastic momentum methods under growth condition
background_latex:
Let $f:\mathbb{R}^d\to\mathbb{R}$ be continuously differentiable, $L$-smooth and $m$-strongly convex (with respect to the Euclidean norm $\|\cdot\|$), with condition number $\kappa:=L/m$, and unique minimizer $x^*\in\arg\min_x f(x)$. A stochastic first-order oracle returns a noisy gradient
\[
 g(x)=\nabla f(x)+\varepsilon(x),
\]
where $\mathbb{E}[\varepsilon(x)\mid x]=0$. The noise is assumed to satisfy the growth condition: there exist constants $\delta\ge 0$ and $\sigma^2\ge 0$ such that for all $x$,
\[
\mathbb{E}\|\varepsilon(x)\|^2 \le \delta\,\|\nabla f(x)\|^2 + \sigma^2.
\]
Consider the following accelerated stochastic gradient-type methods with (constant) algorithm parameters:
1) Nesterov’s accelerated method (NAM), an iteration that uses a momentum parameter and evaluates stochastic gradients at a momentum-extrapolated point.
2) Robust momentum method (RMM), an iteration that updates a momentum variable and a primary iterate using fixed parameters.
3) Implicit accelerated dual averaging (iDAM+), a dual-averaging-based accelerated scheme.
For each method, one is interested in whether it can achieve an accelerated linear bias decay of the form $\exp(-k/c\sqrt{\kappa})$ (or equivalently a per-iteration contraction factor $1-\Theta(1/\sqrt{\kappa})$) under the growth condition when the multiplicative noise level $\delta$ is not small (e.g., $\delta\ge 1$), compared with the non-accelerated $1-\Theta(1/\kappa)$ rate typical of constant-stepsize SGD.
problem_definition_latex:
For NAM, RMM, and iDAM+ under the growth condition, determine (prove) sharp necessary conditions on the multiplicative noise parameter $\delta$ for acceleration over SGD. In particular, establish a lower bound (impossibility result) showing whether there exists a threshold $\delta_0$ such that for some $\delta\ge \delta_0$ and some $(m,L)$-smooth strongly convex objective $f$ together with an oracle satisfying
\(\mathbb{E}\|\varepsilon(x)\|^2 \le \delta\,\|\nabla f(x)\|^2 + \sigma^2\),
no choice of constant algorithm parameters for these methods can guarantee an accelerated bias decay rate $\exp(-k/c\sqrt{\kappa})$ (equivalently contraction $1-\Theta(1/\sqrt{\kappa})$) uniformly over all such problem instances.
latex:
Let $f:\mathbb{R}^d\to\mathbb{R}$ be continuously differentiable, $L$-smooth and $m$-strongly convex (with respect to the Euclidean norm $\|\cdot\|$), with condition number $\kappa:=L/m$, and unique minimizer $x^*\in\arg\min_x f(x)$. A stochastic first-order oracle returns a noisy gradient
\[
 g(x)=\nabla f(x)+\varepsilon(x),
\]
where $\mathbb{E}[\varepsilon(x)\mid x]=0$. The noise is assumed to satisfy the growth condition: there exist constants $\delta\ge 0$ and $\sigma^2\ge 0$ such that for all $x$,
\[
\mathbb{E}\|\varepsilon(x)\|^2 \le \delta\,\|\nabla f(x)\|^2 + \sigma^2.
\]
Consider the following accelerated stochastic gradient-type methods with (constant) algorithm parameters:
1) Nesterov’s accelerated method (NAM), an iteration that uses a momentum parameter and evaluates stochastic gradients at a momentum-extrapolated point.
2) Robust momentum method (RMM), an iteration that updates a momentum variable and a primary iterate using fixed parameters.
3) Implicit accelerated dual averaging (iDAM+), a dual-averaging-based accelerated scheme.
For each method, one is interested in whether it can achieve an accelerated linear bias decay of the form $\exp(-k/c\sqrt{\kappa})$ (or equivalently a per-iteration contraction factor $1-\Theta(1/\sqrt{\kappa})$) under the growth condition when the multiplicative noise level $\delta$ is not small (e.g., $\delta\ge 1$), compared with the non-accelerated $1-\Theta(1/\kappa)$ rate typical of constant-stepsize SGD.

For NAM, RMM, and iDAM+ under the growth condition, determine (prove) sharp necessary conditions on the multiplicative noise parameter $\delta$ for acceleration over SGD. In particular, establish a lower bound (impossibility result) showing whether there exists a threshold $\delta_0$ such that for some $\delta\ge \delta_0$ and some $(m,L)$-smooth strongly convex objective $f$ together with an oracle satisfying
\(\mathbb{E}\|\varepsilon(x)\|^2 \le \delta\,\|\nabla f(x)\|^2 + \sigma^2\),
no choice of constant algorithm parameters for these methods can guarantee an accelerated bias decay rate $\exp(-k/c\sqrt{\kappa})$ (equivalently contraction $1-\Theta(1/\sqrt{\kappa})$) uniformly over all such problem instances.
open_problem_quote: We do not provide a lower bound analysis for NAM, RMM, iDAM+, and, therefore, cannot conclude that these methods do not improve the convergence over SGD when \(\delta \ge 1\). ... One of the limitations of our work is the sharpness of the analysis. Due to the lack of lower bound analysis on \(\delta\), it is unclear whether the condition of having a small \(\delta\) for different methods is necessary for acceleration; and how sharp such a condition is.
counterexample_suitability_rationale: One can search over low-dimensional strongly convex quadratics with parametrized multiplicative-noise oracles to try to exhibit instances where a fixed method/parameterization fails to beat SGD, and performance metrics (contraction factors) are computable. However, proving necessity uniformly over all instances and over all parameter choices is harder than finding a single failing instance.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: Candidate counterexamples can be represented programmatically (e.g., quadratics plus oracle noise models) and evaluated via simulated convergence rates, enabling evolutionary search over instances and parameter settings to maximize the acceleration gap. This can generate strong empirical evidence and conjectures about sharp thresholds in $\delta$, though turning them into rigorous lower bounds still requires theory.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.

literature_review.synthesis:
The source paper (Chen--Na--Kolar, 2023) establishes upper bounds for several constant-parameter accelerated stochastic methods under the growth condition \(\mathbb{E}\|\varepsilon(x)\|^2\le \delta\|\nabla f(x)\|^2+\sigma^2\). It shows that NAM and iDAM+ admit accelerated linear bias decay only for \(\delta<1\), and RMM only for \(\delta<1/4\), whereas DAM+ can be tuned to achieve an accelerated bias contraction \(1-\Theta(1/\sqrt{\kappa})\) for all \(\delta\ge 0\). The paper explicitly notes that these are sufficient conditions and leaves open whether the small-\(\delta\) requirements for NAM/RMM/iDAM+ are necessary, i.e., whether there are sharp impossibility thresholds \(\delta_0\) beyond which no constant-parameter tuning can guarantee acceleration uniformly over \(f\in \mathcal{F}(m,L)\) and growth-condition oracles.

Related works provide partial evidence toward such impossibility results but do not resolve the sharp \(\delta\)-threshold question in the growth-condition model. Counterexample-style papers on momentum in stochastic optimization (e.g., Kidambi--Netrapalli--Jain--Kakade, 2018; Liu--Belkin, 2020) show that heavy-ball/Nesterov-type momentum can fail to accelerate SGD on least-squares-type instances, consistent with the observation that least squares can induce \(\delta\gtrsim 1\). These results motivate the conjecture that for NAM (and likely iDAM+) acceleration is impossible once \(\delta\) exceeds a constant, but they do not provide a sharp necessary condition stated directly in terms of \(\delta\) under the growth condition.

On the algorithmic side, RMM (Cyrus--Hu--Van Scoy--Lessard, 2018) and iDAM+-type methods (Cohen--Diakonikolas--Orecchia, 2018) supply the method definitions and analysis frameworks (IQC/dissipativity and duality-gap viewpoints) used in later growth-condition analyses, while least-squares acceleration results (Jain--Kakade--Kidambi--Netrapalli--Sidford, 2018) illustrate that acceleration is achievable with more tailored schemes even when classical momentum fails. Overall, the state of the art suggests the open problem remains unsolved: sharp lower bounds on \(\delta\) for acceleration of NAM/RMM/iDAM+ under constant parameters and the growth condition are not currently established, and developing such impossibility results (likely via carefully constructed quadratic instances and oracle noise matching the growth condition) is a clear next direction.

sources.title: Convergence Analysis of Accelerated Stochastic Gradient Descent under the Growth Condition
sources.theorem_or_result: Provides upper bounds for accelerated stochastic methods under the growth condition \(\mathbb{E}\|\varepsilon(x)\|^2\le \delta\|\nabla f(x)\|^2+\sigma^2\), showing acceleration for NAM/iDAM+ only when \(\delta<1\) (and for RMM when \(\delta<1/4\)), while DAM+ accelerates for all \(\delta\ge 0\). Explicitly notes absence of lower-bound/impossibility results on \(\delta\) thresholds.
sources.connection: This is the originating paper that formulates the need for sharp necessary conditions on \(\delta\) for acceleration and explicitly leaves lower-bound analyses for NAM/RMM/iDAM+ as open.

sources.title: Accelerating SGD with Momentum for Over-Parameterized Learning
sources.theorem_or_result: Constructs least-squares/over-parameterized settings where classical momentum/Nesterov-type acceleration does not improve over SGD, highlighting fragility of momentum under multiplicative/noise models beyond bounded-variance assumptions.
sources.connection: Supports the plausibility of impossibility thresholds for NAM-like methods when multiplicative noise is large (e.g., least-squares induces \(\delta\gtrsim 1\)), but does not provide a sharp lower bound in terms of \(\delta\) for the growth condition model.

sources.title: On the Insufficiency of Existing Momentum Schemes for Stochastic Optimization
sources.theorem_or_result: Shows that standard momentum schemes (heavy-ball/Nesterov variants) can fail to accelerate SGD on certain stochastic problems, giving counterexamples that separate deterministic acceleration from stochastic behavior.
sources.connection: Provides lower-bound-style counterexamples for momentum acceleration, motivating impossibility results; however, it is not phrased as a sharp \(\delta\)-threshold under the growth condition and does not directly yield necessary conditions for NAM/RMM/iDAM+ with constant parameters.

sources.title: A Robust Accelerated Optimization Algorithm for Strongly Convex Functions
sources.theorem_or_result: Introduces the robust momentum method (RMM) and analyzes deterministic convergence; empirically studies robustness to certain multiplicative noise models.
sources.connection: Defines one of the target algorithms (RMM) for which sharp necessary \(\delta\) thresholds are sought; does not provide impossibility/lower bounds under the growth condition.

sources.title: On the convergence of Nesterov’s accelerated gradient method in stochastic settings
sources.theorem_or_result: Studies stochastic Nesterov acceleration under bounded-variance-type assumptions and characterizes convergence behavior in stochastic regimes.
sources.connection: Provides baseline stochastic NAM analysis under OUBV-like assumptions; does not address growth-condition \(\delta\) thresholds or impossibility results.

sources.title: On Acceleration with Noise-Corrupted Gradients
sources.theorem_or_result: Develops acceleration schemes and analyses under noise-corrupted gradients, highlighting limits and trade-offs between acceleration and noise.
sources.connection: Provides conceptual and technical background for acceleration under stochastic noise and includes iDAM+-related ideas, but does not give sharp \(\delta\)-threshold impossibility results under the growth condition.

sources.title: Accelerating Stochastic Gradient Descent for Least Squares Regression
sources.theorem_or_result: Shows accelerated rates for least-squares regression via specialized acceleration/variance-reduction style methods, contrasting with failures of classical momentum.
sources.connection: Demonstrates that acceleration is possible for least squares but typically requires methods beyond NAM/HB; helps contextualize why impossibility thresholds for NAM/RMM/iDAM+ under growth condition are plausible.
